heading: Mobilization forces: maintenance
label: 10207 Mobilization forces: maintenance
notes: Prior ProvisionsProvisions similar to those in this section were contained in , prior to repeal by .section 276 of this titlePub. L. 103–337, § 1661(a)(2)(A)
Effective DateSection effective , except as otherwise provided, see , set out as a note under .Dec. 1, 1994section 1691 of Pub. L. 103–337section 10001 of this title

children.label: a
children.text: Whenever units or members of the reserve components are ordered to active duty (other than for training) during a period of partial mobilization, the Secretary concerned shall continue to maintain mobilization forces by planning and budgeting for the continued organization and training of the reserve components not mobilized, and make the fullest practicable use of the Federal facilities vacated by mobilized units, consistent with approved joint mobilization plans.

children.label: b
children.text: In this section, the term “partial mobilization” means the mobilization resulting from action by Congress or the President, under any law, to bring units of any reserve component, and members not assigned to units organized to serve as units, to active duty for a limited expansion of the active armed forces.
